goal: Test whether a larger weakly timed chunk manifest can reduce dependence on the small fixed chunk answer list.
reason: The weakly timed data is much more diverse, but the timing noise is too high for the content recognizer to learn useful character alignment.
interpretation: This was not a hardcoded phrase-list improvement; the candidate used an open decoder with zero held-out lexicon coverage.
The weak manifest expanded text variety from a tiny phrase set to hundreds of chunk texts, but weak uniform word timing is not accurate enough for wav2vec+CTC content training.
The current tuned chunked baseline remains the best content checkpoint for now.

The next content improvement should use cleaner labels: either real word/character alignment for more verses, or a two-stage model that learns from high-quality aligned chunks first and uses weak data only after confidence filtering.
